Silver Tickets

A Silver Ticket attack allows an attacker to forge a Kerberos Service Ticket (TGS) after obtaining the password hash of a service account. The forged ticket provides access to a specific Kerberos service without contacting the Domain Controller (KDC), making the attack more stealthy than other Kerberos ticket attacks.

How It Works

  1. The attacker obtains the service account's password hash.
  2. A forged TGS is created and encrypted using that hash.
  3. The ticket can contain arbitrary user and group memberships.
  4. The forged ticket is presented directly to the target service, which validates it locally and, in most environments, does not perform PAC validation with the KDC.

Requirements

  • Service account or machine account password hash.
  • Target service with a registered SPN.
  • Domain SID and target service information.
  • Access to the target service.

Failure Conditions

  • Invalid or outdated service account hash.
  • Service account password has changed.
  • PAC validation or additional Kerberos security controls are enforced.
  • Incorrect domain or SPN information.

Usage Scenarios

  • Accessing a specific service such as IIS, MSSQL, CIFS, or HTTP.
  • Lateral movement after compromising a service account.
  • Maintaining access to a compromised service while avoiding communication with the Domain Controller.

Important Notes

Resources

MITRE ATT&CK T1558.002 CrowdStrike Silver Ticket Attack
